story_summary: Boxwell, a travelling clockmaker, arrives at the rain-soaked Brambled Inn and discovers that the stopped hallway clock may conceal a message.

entities.summary: A patient travelling clockmaker who carries a cedar case of tools.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Boxwell laid his cedar case beside the stopped hallway clock.

entities.summary: A weathered roadside inn with a stopped hallway clock.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The Brambled Inn had kept the same stopped clock for twenty years.

claims.evidence_excerpt: Everyone in three counties knew Boxwell as a clockmaker.
He never travelled without the cedar case.
By dusk, Boxwell reached the Brambled Inn.

chunks.title: Boxwell card
chunks.body: Boxwell is a quiet travelling clockmaker known for patient repairs and a cedar case of tools.

chunks.title: The Brambled Inn
chunks.body: The Brambled Inn is a weathered roadside inn whose hallway clock has been stopped for twenty years.

chunks.title: Open hook from The Cedar Case
chunks.body: The stopped hallway clock at the Brambled Inn may hide a message in its missing escapement spring.

chunks.title: Continuity: The Cedar Case
chunks.body: Previous story summary: Boxwell arrived at the Brambled Inn during heavy rain and found the hallway clock's escapement spring had been removed deliberately.

open_questions: Who removed the escapement spring from the hallway clock?
What message might the stopped clock conceal?
